title: Zhaoanthus rossii
taxonomy: Zhaoanthus rossii belongs to the family of flowering plants and is classified as a species within the genus Zhaoanthus. It is a type of orchid, and as such, is classified in the order Asparagales, and further classified in the family Orchidaceae. Within this family, it is placed in the subfamily Orchidoideae. Zhaoanthus rossii is a rare and endangered species native to China and other parts of Asia. The classification of Zhaoanthus rossii is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.

If we consider the plant "Zanthoxylum bungeanum" or "Zhaoanthus bungeanum," it is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. 

Zanthoxylum bungeanum, also known as Sichuan pepper, is a plant used in traditional Chinese medicine. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum bungeanum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea, and reduce inflammation.

The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum bungeanum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and volatile oils, particularly the compounds hydroxy-alpha-sanshool, which is responsible for its unique numbing sensation. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Zanthoxylum bungeanum include teas, infusions, and powder, often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
